description: |-
  Routes alert signals from monitors and SLOs to notifiers, with per-severity routing and label-matcher-based overrides. If name is set the policy is independent (referenceable by ID); if name is omitted the policy is inline and can only be embedded in another resource (e.g. a bucket).

# chronosphere_notification_policy (Resource)

Routes alert signals from monitors and SLOs to notifiers, with per-severity routing and label-matcher-based overrides. If `name` is set the policy is independent (referenceable by ID); if `name` is omitted the policy is inline and can only be embedded in another resource (e.g. a bucket).

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "chronosphere_team" "platform" {
  name = "Platform"
}

resource "chronosphere_email_alert_notifier" "email" {
  name = "Platform Email"
  to   = "platform@example.com"
}

resource "chronosphere_notification_policy" "platform" {
  name    = "Platform Policy"
  team_id = chronosphere_team.platform.id

  route {
    severity  = "warn"
    notifiers = [chronosphere_email_alert_notifier.email.id]
    group_by {
      label_names = ["service"]
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `name` (String) Display name of the notification policy. If unset, the policy is treated as anonymous/inline and cannot be referenced by ID. Changing between set/unset forces resource replacement.
- `notification_policy_data` (String) Computed/optional JSON serialization of the policy. Primarily used to attach inline policy data to other resources (e.g. buckets).
- `override` (Block List) Ordered overrides that route alerts matching specific label matchers to different destinations. The first matching override is applied; non-matching alerts fall through to the default `route`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--override))
- `route` (Block Set) Per-severity routing rules. Each entry maps a severity (e.g. `warn`, `critical`) to a set of notifiers, destinations, grouping, and repeat behavior. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--route))
- `slug` (String) Stable identifier for the notification policy. Can only be set when `name` is set. Generated from `name` if omitted. Immutable after creation.
- `team_id` (String) ID of the team that owns this notification policy. Required when `name` is set (anonymous policies cannot be owned).

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `is_independent` (Boolean) Read-only internal marker tracking whether the policy is independent (named) or inline. Used to force replacement when transitioning between the two.

<a id="nestedblock--override"></a>
### Nested Schema for `override`

Required:

- `alert_label_matcher` (Block List, Min: 1) List of label matchers used to select a subset of series. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--override--alert_label_matcher))

Optional:

- `route` (Block Set) Per-severity routing rules. Each entry maps a severity (e.g. `warn`, `critical`) to a set of notifiers, destinations, grouping, and repeat behavior. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--override--route))

<a id="nestedblock--override--alert_label_matcher"></a>
### Nested Schema for `override.alert_label_matcher`

Required:

- `name` (String) Label name to match.
- `type` (String) Match operator: one of `=`, `!=`, `=~` (regex), `!~` (regex negation).
- `value` (String) Label value (or regex pattern, for regex matchers) to match against.


<a id="nestedblock--override--route"></a>
### Nested Schema for `override.route`

Required:

- `severity` (String) Severity this route applies to (e.g. `warn`, `critical`). Case-sensitive.

Optional:

- `destination` (Block List) Inline notification destinations defined directly on the route. Each block sets at most one of `slack`, `pagerduty`, `webhook`, `ops_genie`, `victor_ops`, or `email`. Cannot be combined with `notifiers`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--override--route--destination))
- `disable_repeat` (Boolean) If true, alerts are notified once and on state changes (new firing alerts, resolves) but are never re-sent on the `repeat_interval` timer. Defaults to false.
- `group_by` (Block List, Max: 1) Optional grouping configuration controlling how alerts are batched before delivery. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--override--route--group_by))
- `notifiers` (Set of String) Slugs of notifier resources that receive alerts at this severity. Cannot be combined with `destination`.
- `repeat_interval` (String) How often to resend unresolved alerts at this severity (e.g. `4h`). Ignored when `disable_repeat` is true.

<a id="nestedblock--override--route--destination"></a>
### Nested Schema for `override.route.destination`

Optional:

- `disable_resolves` (Boolean) If true, do not send notifications when alerts resolve. Defaults to false.
- `email` (Block List, Max: 1) Email delivery configuration for this destination. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--override--route--destination--email))
- `ops_genie` (Block List, Max: 1) OpsGenie delivery configuration for this destination. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--override--route--destination--ops_genie))
- `pagerduty` (Block List, Max: 1) PagerDuty delivery configuration for this destination. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--override--route--destination--pagerduty))
- `slack` (Block List, Max: 1) Slack delivery configuration for this destination. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--override--route--destination--slack))
- `victor_ops` (Block List, Max: 1) VictorOps (Splunk On-Call) delivery configuration for this destination. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--override--route--destination--victor_ops))
- `webhook` (Block List, Max: 1) Generic webhook delivery configuration for this destination. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--override--route--destination--webhook))

<a id="nestedblock--override--route--destination--email"></a>
### Nested Schema for `override.route.destination.email`

Required:

- `addresses` (List of String) Email addresses to deliver notifications to.


<a id="nestedblock--override--route--destination--ops_genie"></a>
### Nested Schema for `override.route.destination.ops_genie`

Required:

- `external_connection_slug` (String) Slug of the OpsGenie external connection holding the integration credentials.


<a id="nestedblock--override--route--destination--pagerduty"></a>
### Nested Schema for `override.route.destination.pagerduty`

Required:

- `external_connection_slug` (String) Slug of the PagerDuty external connection holding the integration credentials.


<a id="nestedblock--override--route--destination--slack"></a>
### Nested Schema for `override.route.destination.slack`

Required:

- `external_connection_slug` (String) Slug of the Slack external connection holding the integration credentials.

Optional:

- `channels` (List of String) Slack channels to send notifications to.


<a id="nestedblock--override--route--destination--victor_ops"></a>
### Nested Schema for `override.route.destination.victor_ops`

Required:

- `external_connection_slug` (String) Slug of the VictorOps external connection holding the integration credentials.
- `routing_keys` (List of String) VictorOps routing keys identifying the destination escalation policies.


<a id="nestedblock--override--route--destination--webhook"></a>
### Nested Schema for `override.route.destination.webhook`

Required:

- `external_connection_slug` (String) Slug of the webhook external connection holding the endpoint URL and auth settings.

Optional:

- `query_parameter` (Block List) Additional query parameters appended to the webhook URL when delivering this notification. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--override--route--destination--webhook--query_parameter))

<a id="nestedblock--override--route--destination--webhook--query_parameter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `override.route.destination.webhook.query_parameter`

Required:

- `key` (String) Query parameter name.
- `value` (String) Query parameter value.




<a id="nestedblock--override--route--group_by"></a>
### Nested Schema for `override.route.group_by`

Optional:

- `label_names` (List of String) Label names to group alerts by. Alerts with identical values for these labels are bundled into a single notification.




<a id="nestedblock--route"></a>
### Nested Schema for `route`

Required:

- `severity` (String) Severity this route applies to (e.g. `warn`, `critical`). Case-sensitive.

Optional:

- `destination` (Block List) Inline notification destinations defined directly on the route. Each block sets at most one of `slack`, `pagerduty`, `webhook`, `ops_genie`, `victor_ops`, or `email`. Cannot be combined with `notifiers`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--route--destination))
- `disable_repeat` (Boolean) If true, alerts are notified once and on state changes (new firing alerts, resolves) but are never re-sent on the `repeat_interval` timer. Defaults to false.
- `group_by` (Block List, Max: 1) Optional grouping configuration controlling how alerts are batched before delivery. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--route--group_by))
- `notifiers` (Set of String) Slugs of notifier resources that receive alerts at this severity. Cannot be combined with `destination`.
- `repeat_interval` (String) How often to resend unresolved alerts at this severity (e.g. `4h`). Ignored when `disable_repeat` is true.

<a id="nestedblock--route--destination"></a>

Optional:

- `disable_resolves` (Boolean) If true, do not send notifications when alerts resolve. Defaults to false.
- `email` (Block List, Max: 1) Email delivery configuration for this destination. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--route--destination--email))
- `ops_genie` (Block List, Max: 1) OpsGenie delivery configuration for this destination. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--route--destination--ops_genie))
